title: ResearchCode-Agent Evaluation Case
description: Schema for a single evaluation question with gold-standard annotations. v1.4.0 adds snapshot-aware validation fields. Validation rules: (1) indexed_in_system=true entities must exist in the scan snapshot; (2) indexed_in_system=false entities must have missing_reason and source_evidence; (3) system_answerable=full requires all required nodes/edges to have indexed_in_system=true; (4) chain_complete=true requires all required edges to have indexed_in_system=true.

question_fingerprint.description: Normalized hash of question text for duplicate detection across splits

task_type.description: Expected task type for routing evaluation

source_answerable.description: Whether the question can be answered by reading the source code at the fixed commit (source truth level)

system_answerable.description: Whether ResearchCode-Agent's current indexed data can fully, partially, or not at all answer this question (indexed observation level). 'full' = all evidence in index; 'partial' = some evidence missing (e.g. CALLS_METHOD edge); 'insufficient' = key evidence absent from index

gold_status.description: Current verification status of the gold standard

execution_status.description: Whether this record can be executed in the current evaluation run. 'executable' = all inputs ready; 'prepared_not_executed' = question ready but base snapshot not built; 'executed' = already run; 'excluded' = not for formal metrics

evaluation_layers.description: Which evaluation layers apply to this question

expected_task_type.description: Expected routing result (may differ from task_type if testing misrouting)

entity_type.description: Entity type (java_class, java_method, backend_api, frontend_api_call, python_class, python_function)

relevance.description: Relevance level for retrieval evaluation. must_recall: must be found by index-level retrieval. acceptable_alternative: acceptable index-level match. insufficient: related but not sufficient. distractor: intentionally misleading. source_only: exists in source truth but not in index; excluded from index-level Recall.

indexed_in_system.description: Whether this entity exists in ResearchCode-Agent's current scan snapshot (indexed observation). true = entity found in scan results; false = entity exists in source truth but is missing from the index (e.g. due to parser limitations, dynamic URL patterns, or interface-type mismatches). When false, this entity is excluded from index-level Recall computation and the relevance should be 'source_only'.

missing_reason.description: Why this entity is not present in the scan index. Required when indexed_in_system=false. Examples: 'dynamic_url', 'interface_type_mismatch', 'component_not_recognized', 'parser_limitation'.

file_path.description: Relative path within the repository where the entity can be verified in source code

start_line.description: 1-based start line of the entity in source code

end_line.description: 1-based end line of the entity in source code

source_evidence.description: Source code location where this entity can be verified at the fixed commit. Required when indexed_in_system=false.

category.description: File's role in a change plan

reason.description: Why this file is in this category

gold_files.description: Gold-standard file sets for change plan evaluation

runtime_entity_id.description: Database auto-increment ID from a specific scan run; not stable across rescans

indexed_in_system.description: Whether this node exists in ResearchCode-Agent's current scan snapshot (indexed observation). true = node found in scan results; false = node exists in source truth but is missing from the index. When any required node has indexed_in_system=false, chain_complete must be false and system_answerable must not be 'full'.

missing_reason.description: Why this node is not present in the scan index. Required when indexed_in_system=false. Examples: 'dynamic_url', 'interface_type_mismatch', 'component_not_recognized', 'parser_limitation'.

gold_nodes.description: Expected graph nodes for trace chain evaluation

indexed_in_system.description: Whether this edge exists in ResearchCode-Agent's current indexed data (indexed observation). true = edge found in scan results; false = edge exists in source truth but is missing from the index (e.g. CALLS_METHOD for interface-typed services). When false, this edge counts as a false negative in graph retrieval evaluation.

gold_edges.description: Expected graph edges for trace chain evaluation

chain_complete.description: Whether the gold chain is expected to be fully resolvable

claim.description: A factual claim that must appear in the answer

evidence_file.description: File path containing evidence for this claim

file_path.description: File path containing the evidence range

start_line.description: Start line of the evidence range

end_line.description: End line of the evidence range

required_claims.description: Factual claims that must be present in a correct answer

optional_claims.description: Claims that may appear but are not required

claim.description: A claim that must NOT appear in the answer

reason.description: Why this claim is incorrect or unsupported

description.description: What the system should report as uncertain

required_risks.description: Risks that must be mentioned in change plan answers

forbidden_recommendations.description: Recommendations that must NOT appear in change plan answers

expected_language.description: Expected answer language (should match question language)

change_plan_type.description: Type of change plan gold standard

annotation_type.description: Whether this change plan is from a real historical commit or an expert-constructed hypothetical

base_commit_sha.description: The starting commit SHA for the historical change

target_commit_sha.description: The ending commit SHA for the historical change

change_request.description: The original change request or issue description

observed_changed_files.description: Files that were actually changed between base and target commits

source_commit_message.description: The commit message of the target commit

diff_summary.description: A summary of the diff between base and target commits

change_plan_provenance.description: Provenance information for change plan gold standards, describing whether the case comes from a historical commit pair or an expert-constructed hypothetical

special_sample_type.description: Which special sample types (1-20) this question represents

annotator.description: Agent role that created the annotation (e.g. 'evidence_annotator_A')

reviewer.description: Agent role that reviewed the annotation (e.g. 'adversarial_reviewer_A')

disagreements.description: Recorded disagreements between annotator and reviewer

source_files.description: Repository files used as evidence for gold standard

source_lines.description: Specific line ranges used as evidence

generation_method.description: How the gold standard was generated (e.g. 'manual_code_inspection', 'parser_output_verified')
